header.location: Portland, OR (Open to Remote or Relocation)

summary: I'm Jamie—a curious, people-loving (97% extrovert!) Learning & Development professional who thrives on solving problems through data analysis, developing enablement programs grounded in evidence, and collaborating with stakeholders for sustainable impact. Expect me to always be seeking ways we can close performance gaps and ramp teams faster—through human-centered, data-driven approaches.

bullets.text: Manage {prog|enablement programs} end-to-end for 20+ initiatives, partnering with Marketing, Tech, and Sales teams to close {data|performance gaps}
{fac|Deliver training} webinars to 600+ participants and build {id|curriculum} across live sessions, async materials, and SOPs
Serve as {id|subject-matter} owner for documentation and knowledge systems for 70+ staff, reducing onboarding time by 75%
Drive adoption of a new process by interviewing users to diagnose {data|gaps}, then designing targeted interventions that lifted usage
Led an Inclusive Leadership {change|change-management} initiative using ADKAR, scaled from a pilot to 23 global locations
Built awareness, found internal champions, and ran reinforcement workshops to sustain {change|adoption} in a change-heavy environment
Partnered with a Denmark DEI specialist as {id|SME} to build {id|curriculum}, practice exercises, and follow-up coaching
Optimized onboarding for new hires across 3 global locations through process analysis and clearer responsibilities
Led a manager effectiveness initiative, transforming {data|feedback data} into reports that informed training strategy
Analyzed 2,000+ employee experiences to {data|measure} drivers and guide C-suite decisions
Provided consultations and internal {prog|training} on DEI, Inclusive Leadership, and performance review
Delivered 5 {cross|cross-functional} projects spanning survey development, rewards & recognition, and L&D
Developed proficiency across HR functions, from recruitment and compensation to performance management
Drafted job descriptions and supporting materials for a challenging international role
Engaged global talent via personalized {cross|communication} and customer service, increasing applications by 566% in 2 months
Organized events and {fac|moderated panels} with C-suite at top 3 universities, reaching 230+ students
As a team of consultants, partnered with an HR client to audit 300+ leave cases, quantifying $362K to refute assumptions
Empowered the client team to present executive-ready {data|data} insights, driving policy redesigns and training

bullets: Relevant Coursework: Organizational Psychology, Research Methods, Group Dynamics & Leadership, Consumer Psychology

Scholarships: Freeman Asian Scholarship (4-year full-tuition), Tuck Bridge Scholarship (full)

Data & PM Tools: Data Analysis (Excel, Google Sheets, SPSS) · Project Management (Asana, Airtable) · CRM (HubSpot)

jd.meta: Samsara — Enablement Business Partner, Customer Success

changeLog: 2026-05-31: Tailored for Samsara — Enablement Business Partner, Customer Success (Remote US). Broad umbrella 'Learning & Development.' Location → Portland, OR (Open to Remote — role excludes many metros but NOT Portland/OR). Led InGenius with enablement programs + performance-gap closing + training delivery + curriculum + SME ownership. Vestas surfaces the ADKAR change-management story heavily (JD wants change-mgmt + curriculum dev in change-heavy environments). Skills include Notion/SharePoint (knowledge mgmt) — JD names Articulate/Absorb/Showpad/Jira which Jamie lacks, so not claimed. ODN collective. Counts fixed (InGenius/Vestas/NextGen/Kronos=4, ODN=2). NOTE: JD requires post-sale CSM/AM experience — Jamie does NOT have this; cover is honest, leans on enablement + change-mgmt strengths.
